meaning: n.货摊,畜栏,厩,出售摊v.(使)停转,(使)停止,迟延
examples: He sold boots at a market stall.
Another discovered a valuable diamond brooch covered in tar in a bathroom stall.
See if you can stall her while I finish searching her office.
This could stall order growth or result in postponements or cancelations in existing backlog.
Upon reaching the banks, the herbivore ranks stall hesitantly, knowing full well that Nile crocodiles lie waiting.
